changes.old: If we show you ads, depending on your settings, we will use your information across the account(s) in your Accounts Centre to show you ads, including using cookies, to personalize your ads on and off the Meta Company Products, including apps using Meta Audience Network, and measure how those ads perform.
changes.new: If we show you ads, we use data that advertisers and other partners provide us about your activity off Meta Company Products, including websites and apps, to show you better ads. You can control whether we use this data to show you ads in your ad settings.
changes.explanation: The updated policy indicates that your data may now be shared with additional third parties, such as partners, advertisers, service providers, or group companies.
changes.suggested_action: Check which third parties are involved and whether you can opt out of certain types of sharing or limit data transfers in your account settings.

changes.new: We advertise our own products and services on non-Meta ad platforms. We use marketing vendors to deliver these ads. If we use your activity on our Products to tailor these ads, you can opt out of this type of use.
changes.explanation: The updated policy indicates that your data may now be shared with additional third parties, such as partners, advertisers, service providers, or group companies.
changes.suggested_action: Check which third parties are involved and whether you can opt out of certain types of sharing or limit data transfers in your account settings.

changes.new: A third party that creates reports to help advertisers understand how their content and ads are performing. These reports also measure whether people took an action after seeing an ad.
changes.explanation: The updated policy indicates that your data may now be shared with additional third parties, such as partners, advertisers, service providers, or group companies.

changes.new: Transferring, storing or processing your information across borders, including from and to the United States and other countries: We share information we collect globally, both internally across our offices and data centers and externally with our [partners\[91\]](https://mbasic.facebook.com/privacy/policy/printable/?entry point=POLICY SUBSECTION%3A19.1-TheCategoriesOfInformation#annotation-91), [third parties\[92\]](https://mbasic.facebook.com/privacy/policy/printable/?entry point=POLICY SUBSECTION%3A19.1-TheCategoriesOfInformation#annotation-92) and [service providers\[93\]](https://mbasic.facebook.com/privacy/policy/printable/?entry point=POLICY SUBSECTION%3A19.1-TheCategoriesOfInformation#annotation-93). Because Meta is global, with users, partners, vendors and employees around the world, transfers are necessary:
changes.explanation: The updated policy indicates that your data may now be shared with additional third parties, such as partners, advertisers, service providers, or group companies.
changes.suggested_action: Check which third parties are involved and whether you can opt out of certain types of sharing or limit data transfers in your account settings.

changes.old: Personalising the Meta Products: Our systems automatically process information we have collected and stored associated with you and others to assess and understand your interests and your preferences and provide you personalised experiences on and across the Meta Company Products in accordance with our terms. This is how we:
changes.new: Providing and improving our Meta Products: The provision of the Meta Products includes collecting, storing, and, where relevant, sharing, profiling, reviewing and curating, and in some instances not only automated processing but also manual (human) reviewing, to:
changes.explanation: The updated policy indicates expanded tracking or behavioural analytics (e.g., interactions, pages visited, search terms, click activity, or location) which may be used for personalisation or profiling.
changes.suggested_action: Review privacy settings to limit tracking/analytics. Consider disabling personalised ads, restricting cookies, or using privacy tools if concerned about behavioural profiling.

changes.old: Meta will combine your information across accounts on Meta Company Products and use your information as described in this section. This includes using your information to keep you and others safe, more accurately count people and measure how they use our products, and make sure our Products are working correctly so we can fix them if they’re not.
changes.new: How is your information shared on Meta Products or with integrated partners?
changes.explanation: The updated policy indicates that your data may now be shared with additional third parties, such as partners, advertisers, service providers, or group companies.
changes.suggested_action: Check which third parties are involved and whether you can opt out of certain types of sharing or limit data transfers in your account settings.

changes.old: WhatsApp will combine your information across accounts on Meta Company Products and use your information as described in the WhatsApp in Accounts Center Supplemental Privacy Policy.
changes.new: To associate your accounts on Meta Company Products with your WhatsApp account in the same Accounts Center and share your information with WhatsApp.

changes.new: You can use the Digital Advertising Alliance industry opt-out platform on your web browser. If you live in Canada, you can use the Digital Advertising Alliance of Canada industry opt-out platform. Certain ad blockers and tools to restrict cookies can interfere with your ability to opt out.
changes.explanation: The updated policy introduces or expands the purposes for which your data is used (e.g., advertising, analytics, research, or security) or clarifies the legal basis for processing.
changes.suggested_action: Check whether you are comfortable with these purposes and, where applicable, adjust your consent or opt-out preferences.

changes.new: For example, if you make a purchase from an Instagram shop using checkout, the shop will receive information to complete your transaction. This may include your order items, your contact details and shipping information. If the shop uses a payment services provider, such as PayPal, to facilitate the transaction, the provider will receive the transaction amount, a transaction description (to appear on your credit card statement) and your payment card information, such as cardholder name, card number, expiration date and billing address. Learn more about payments on Instagram.
changes.explanation: The updated policy introduces or changes billing/payment-related terms (subscriptions, fees, pricing, or payment methods).
changes.suggested_action: Review these financial terms carefully to understand any new costs or obligations.

changes.old: Under applicable data protection law, companies must have a legal basis to process personal data. When we talk about "processing personal data," we mean the ways we collect, use and share your information, as we described in the other sections of this Policy above.
changes.new: Processing information subject to special protections under applicable laws that you provide so we can share it with those you choose, to provide, personalize and improve our Products and to undertake analytics. We’ll collect, store, publish and apply automated, or sometimes manual (human), processing for these purposes.
changes.explanation: The updated policy introduces or expands the purposes for which your data is used (e.g., advertising, analytics, research, or security) or clarifies the legal basis for processing.

changes.new: Things we infer about you, like topics we think you may be interested in
changes.explanation: This section of the policy text has been modified.
changes.suggested_action: Read this part of the policy carefully to understand how it affects your data.

changes.old: We process information if it will protect you or prevent harm, like saving your life or someone else’s.
changes.new: We use information we collect to help protect people from harm and provide safe, secure Products.
changes.explanation: The updated policy describes new or enhanced security measures to protect your data, such as encryption, access controls, or monitoring.
changes.suggested_action: This may improve protection of your data. You can still review details to understand what changed.

changes.old: When you use our Products, you see ads, like product listings in Shops. Meta personalizes your ads on and across Meta Company Products. You also see ads shown through Meta Audience Network when you visit other apps. To provide our Products for free, we use your information to show you ads, which advertisers, businesses, organizations and others pay us to show you. We want everything you see to be interesting and useful to you, so we personalize ads for you and measure how they perform. We use information from your account or across accounts in your Accounts Center and cookies on our Products for this. You can control whether we show you ads and which information we use to show you ads in Ad Preferences.
changes.new: When you use our Products, you see ads and sponsored or commercial content, like product listings in Shops. Meta personalizes your ads on and across Meta Company Products. You also see ads shown through Meta Audience Network or commercial content when you visit other apps. We use your information to show you ads paid for by advertisers, businesses, and organizations. We want everything you see to be interesting and useful to you.
changes.explanation: This section of the policy text has been modified.
changes.suggested_action: Read this part of the policy carefully to understand how it affects your data.

changes.new: If you add accounts to the same Accounts Center, we also use your information to offer connected experiences and improve your experiences across accounts.
